q: A dicotyledonous stem cross-section shows which arrangement of vascular tissue?
options: Xylem and phloem scattered randomly throughout the stem parenchyma
A central solid core of xylem surrounded by a ring of phloem
Vascular bundles arranged in a ring near the periphery, with xylem internal and phloem external, separated by cambium
Phloem located in the center (pith) with xylem surrounding it in a hollow cylinder
explanation: In dicot stems, vascular bundles form a ring; within each bundle, xylem is on the inner side and phloem on the outer side, with cambium between them allowing secondary growth.
